'''Paul Johnson''' (born 25 November 1978) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England Knights|Emerging England]] and [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]], and at club level for the [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref name="Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|title=Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327102545/http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|url-status=dead}}</ref> the [[Bradford Bulls]], the [[Warrington Wolves]], the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]], as a {{rlp|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Johnson was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

==Club career==
===Wigan Warriors===
Johnson made his professional début from the bench as a 17-year-old for Wigan in 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/print.php?type=A&item_id=592 |title = Wigan Warriors Rugby League Fan Site - www.cherryandwhite.co.uk}}</ref>

He played for Wigan from the interchange bench in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

Johnson played for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] from the interchange bench in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] loss to [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]].

Johnson played for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] from the interchange bench in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]].

===Bradford Bulls===
Johnson joined Bradford from the Wigan club in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |title=Johnson makes Bulls switch |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3255140.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=30 October 2024 |date=3 December 2003}}</ref> Having won [[Super League VIII]], Bradford played against 2003 NRL Premiers, the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]]. Johnson played as a {{rlp|ce}} in Bradford's 22–4 victory. He played for Bradford as a {{rlp|ce}} in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Leeds.

The following year he played for Bradford as a {{rlp|sr}}  in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the Leeds.

As [[Super League]] champions Bradford faced [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Johnson played as a {{rlp|sr}}  in Bradford's 30–10 victory.

===Later career===
Johnson joined [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] from the Bradford side in 2007.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Johnson heads for Bulls' exit door |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/jul/06/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=30 October 2024 |date=6 July 2006}}</ref>

In October 2010, Johnson was signed by [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] after being released by Wakefield.<ref>{{cite web |title=Crusaders sign Wakefield Trinity Wildcats' Paul Johnson |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/celtic_crusaders/9075067.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=30 October 2024 |date=8 October 2010}}</ref> He made his final Super League appearance in September 2011 against former club Wigan, announcing his retirement shortly after the game.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby league: Crusaders bow out with a heavy defeat as fans wonder what lies ahead |url=https://www.dailypost.co.uk/news/local-news/rugby-league-crusaders-bow-out-2684992 |website=North Wales Live |access-date=30 October 2024 |date=15 September 2011}}</ref>

==International career==
In 1998, Johnson played for [[England national rugby league team|Emerging England]] in a 15–12 victory over Wales.<ref>{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Elder statesmen negotiate England escape|newspaper=The Times|date=20 July 1998|page=32|publisher=News International Trading Limited|location=London|id={{ProQuest|317976921}}}}</ref>

Johnson made his Great Britain début in 2001, went on to play in the 2004 and 2005 [[Rugby League Tri-Nations]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Johnson was voted 'Back of the Series' against Australia in 2001.

Johnson was then selected in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain team]] to compete in the end of season [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] he played from the interchange bench in the Lions' 44–4 loss.

He missed a large part of the 2005's [[Super League X]] with shoulder and thumb injuries but was in sensational form on his return and gained selection for the Tri Nations tournament, scoring a [[hat-trick]] against New Zealand.

==Personal life==
In February 2003 his younger brother, Wigan academy player Craig Johnson, was killed in a car crash along with Billy-Joe Edwards, a promising Wigan academy player and younger brother of former Wigan player [[Shaun Edwards]].<ref name="Rugby league players killed in crash">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/uk_news/england/2741899.stm|title=Rugby league players killed in crash|publisher=BBC|date=14 February 2003|access-date=20 February 2008}}</ref>
